_comment: Tool-facing hardware/address declaration for the Donkey Kong board.
SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the shared arcade-js Python tools: they read this
file (via --hardware) instead of hardcoding any DK addresses, so tools/ stays
game-agnostic. The ENGINE keeps its own numeric constants in boards/dkong/
{memory,io}.js and games/dkong/manifest.js (the pixel-validated load path is
NOT refactored to import JSON); a drift-test in boards/dkong/test/board.test.js
asserts this file matches those engine constants so they can never diverge.
Numbers are decimal (standard JSON has no 0x); '_hex' fields are annotations
the loader ignores. Values copied verbatim from boards/dkong/memory.js,
_controlByte_hex: 0x3E -- ROM 0x0000, first byte of the reset vector (LD A,n); config-probe control

z80Reset._hex: AF=0x0040 (MAME sets Z flag), IX=IY=0xFFFF after reset, rest 0

landmarks.name: boot init done, NMI enable at 0x02B8 fetched
landmarks.provenance: Measured: PC-exact read tap on 0x02B8 (the NMI-enable point) fires at cycle 180796. Corroborates an independently derived 180816 to within 20 cycles; the small gap is fetch-of-0x02B8 vs completion.

landmarks.name: first vblank NMI fires
landmarks.provenance: Measured: read tap on the NMI vector 0x0066. Supersedes an earlier DERIVED figure of 198144, which assumed the NMI asserts at vblank start (46080 into the frame). It does not: every NMI lands at frame N.000x, i.e. AT the frame boundary, because MAME's frame origin for this driver IS the vblank point. The 10-21 cycle spread is the CPU finishing the current instruction before taking the interrupt.
